capabilities: Partition tables using a key that optimizes query performance and data distribution, ensuring scalability and efficient data access.

procedure.action: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the 10 slowest queries and extract columns appearing in WHERE clauses across >60% of queries to identify potential partition keys.
Invoke CREATE TABLE with PARTITION BY clause to define partitions based on the selected key, ensuring even data distribution.

Initialize query planner to leverage partition pruning by setting enable_partition_pruning = on in the database configuration by loading environment-specific values from config file; assert all required keys are present via config_validate(); assert config_validation_error_count < 1.
Schedule regular ANALYZE operations on partitioned tables to update statistics and maintain query performance.

Invoke a partition lifecycle management script to archive or drop partitions older than a specified retention period following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.

summary: Partitioning data by a chosen key enables databases to efficiently scale by distributing data across partitions. This approach allows queries to target only relevant partitions, improving performance through data pruning and reducing unnecessary scans.
title: Key-Based Data Partitioning for Scalable Query Pruning

failureConditions: Inefficient partition key selection leads to uneven data distribution; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Queries do not leverage partition pruning, resulting in full table scans; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Outdated statistics cause suboptimal query plans; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
metrics: all required keys are present via config_validate()
test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
successConditions: Verify that p95 query execution time decreases by at least 30% after partitioning; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Check that partitioned tables have a balanced row count across partitions; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Ensure that partition pruning reduces scanned partitions by at least 50% for targeted queries; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
